premise:
The Meridian Gallery heist was supposed to be routine—a simple data extraction from their client database. But when the crew's interface specialist vanished three days before the job, veteran handler Maya Chen found herself with a problem: a custom security system that changes its layout every six hours, and no one left who speaks its language. Enter you, the newest addition to Chen's crew. You've got the basics down, but building the dynamic interface tools needed to crack Meridian's adaptive defenses? That's going to require some creative problem-solving.

The target is a high-end art gallery that's really a front for money laundering. Their security system rebuilds its user interface in real-time, making it nearly impossible to crack with static tools. Your job is to construct a flexible, component-based infiltration kit that can adapt as quickly as their defenses change. The crew has forty-eight hours to prep, and Chen's reputation—along with a seven-figure payday—hangs in the balance.
worldRules: Every interface component must be reusable and modular—no single-use tools
The security system adapts every six hours, so your tools must adapt faster
Each crew member's specialty is essential; the job fails if anyone can't deliver
Information flows through secure channels only—no direct communication during execution
The plan succeeds or fails based on how well the components work together
centralQuestion: Can you build a modular interface system that adapts faster than Meridian's defenses?

protagonist.name: Alex
protagonist.personality: Methodical but eager to prove themselves, asks good questions
protagonist.motivation: Wants to earn their place on Chen's crew and prove they can handle complex jobs
protagonist.arc: From nervous newcomer following instructions to confident specialist designing solutions the whole crew depends on

guide.name: Maya Chen
guide.personality: Dry, precise, and unflappable—delivers devastating observations with perfect calm
guide.motivation: Maintains her reputation for impossible jobs by ensuring every crew member performs flawlessly
guide.relationship: Pragmatic mentor who assigns real responsibility and expects professional results

supportingCast.name: Viktor
supportingCast.personality: Perfectionist who thinks in blueprints and contingencies
supportingCast.motivation: Designs elegant solutions that account for every possible failure mode

supportingCast.name: Sam
supportingCast.personality: Practical and direct, focused on what works under pressure
supportingCast.motivation: Needs tools that won't fail when the plan inevitably goes sideways

supportingCast.name: Riley
supportingCast.personality: Detail-oriented pattern spotter who catches what others miss
supportingCast.motivation: Provides the intelligence that makes the impossible jobs possible

antagonist.name: Meridian Adaptive Security Protocol
antagonist.personality: Relentless, unpredictable, learns from every intrusion attempt
antagonist.motivation: Protect the gallery's data by staying one step ahead of any infiltration tools

locations.name: The Workshop
locations.description: Chen's converted warehouse space filled with monitors, component libraries, and testing rigs
locations.storyFunction: Where Alex learns to build and test modular interface components

locations.name: Meridian Gallery
locations.description: Sleek art gallery with hidden servers and adaptive security nodes throughout
locations.storyFunction: The target location where the final infiltration takes place

locations.name: Mobile Command
locations.description: Surveillance van parked three blocks from Meridian, packed with communication gear
locations.storyFunction: Mission control during execution, where the crew coordinates the live operation

locations.name: The Simulation Lab
locations.description: Isolated testing environment that mimics Meridian's security behavior
locations.storyFunction: Safe space to rehearse component integration and test failure scenarios

locations.name: Rooftop Extraction
locations.description: Emergency exit point with clear sightlines and multiple escape routes
locations.storyFunction: Final location where success or failure becomes clear

visualStyle: Sharp contrasts between the warm amber glow of workshop monitors and the cold blue security lighting of the target. Technical blueprints and component diagrams overlay dark environments. Clean geometric interfaces float against textured backgrounds of concrete, steel, and glass. Color coding distinguishes different system states—green for functional components, red for triggered alarms, amber for adaptive changes in progress.
